creator: mm@FreeBSD.org
text:
Well, its true that it may get defined, but you didn't consider the
standard situation in your patch. This does not work.
Where should the Makefile get default values for MAKEOBJDIRPREFIX and
SRC_BASE?

Here is a revised patch, maintainer please approve:

Index: ports/sysutils/fusefs-kmod/files/patch-fuse_module_Makefile
===================================================================
RCS file:
/home/pcvs/ports/sysutils/fusefs-kmod/files/patch-fuse_module_Makefile,v
retrieving revision 1.3
diff -u -r1.3 patch-fuse_module_Makefile
--- ports/sysutils/fusefs-kmod/files/patch-fuse_module_Makefile    22
Aug 2007 11:38:05 -0000    1.3
+++ ports/sysutils/fusefs-kmod/files/patch-fuse_module_Makefile    5 Sep
2007 14:13:54 -0000
@@ -1,12 +1,18 @@
 --- fuse_module/Makefile.orig    2007-06-19 14:35:22.000000000 +0200
 +++ fuse_module/Makefile    2007-08-16 18:31:50.000000000 +0200
-@@ -21,7 +21,8 @@
+@@ -21,7 +21,14 @@
  .endif
 
  .if defined(KERNCONF)
 -KERNCONFDIR= /usr/obj/usr/src/sys/${KERNCONF}
++.if !defined(MAKEOBJDIRPREFIX)
++MAKEOBJDIRPREFIX=/usr/obj
++.endif
++.if !defined(SRC_BASE)
++SRC_BASE=/usr/src
++.endif
 +KERNCONF1!= echo ${KERNCONF} | sed -e 's/ .*//g'
-+KERNCONFDIR= /usr/obj/usr/src/sys/${KERNCONF1}
++KERNCONFDIR= ${MAKEOBJDIRPREFIX}${SRC_BASE}/sys/${KERNCONF1}
  .endif
 
  .if defined(KERNCONFDIR)
